mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real savings and loan balances combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust household balance sheets and purchasing power, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS rises and CPIAUCSL is stable, with out-of-sample accuracy below 55% over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 in post-shock recovery windows, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of accelerating INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer demand and corporate earnings, producing positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction effects show no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate strong residential construction demand and broader economic resilience, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of high HOUST observations, or the series shows Rank IC < 0.03 across all rolling 3-year test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent during late-cycle expansions signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of low-GDP trigger instances, or the hypothesis fails to maintain Rank IC > 0.03 in late-cycle regimes.
